title: The Benefits of Reading
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Reading remains one of the most (0) beneficial activities a person can engage in, regardless of age. Books provide a window into other worlds, firing the (17) imagination and transporting readers to times and places they may never otherwise experience. Great literature is (18) wonderful in its ability to capture human emotion and experience in just a few lines. In addition to entertainment, books are an invaluable source of (19) knowledge that can inform, educate, and inspire. Reading regularly appears to satisfy an intellectual (20) curiosity that other forms of media rarely match. People who read frequently tend to show a noticeable (21) improvement in vocabulary, writing skills, and critical thinking. For many people, reading is also a form of (22) relaxation that provides a welcome escape from daily pressures. Research confirms that the (23) significant cognitive benefits of reading continue into old age. Even in a world dominated by screens and social media, reading remains a deeply (24) enjoyable and rewarding pursuit.

example.text: Reading remains one of the most (0) BENEFICIAL activities a person can engage in, regardless of age. (BENEFIT)

questions.explanation: A noun is needed after 'the'; 'imagine' becomes 'imagination' by replacing '-ne' with '-nation'.
An adjective is needed after the verb 'is'; 'wonder' becomes 'wonderful' by adding the suffix '-ful'.
A noun is needed after 'source of'; 'know' becomes 'knowledge' by adding the suffix '-ledge'.
A noun is needed after 'intellectual'; 'curious' becomes 'curiosity' by changing '-ious' to '-ity'.
A noun is needed after 'a noticeable'; 'improve' becomes 'improvement' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
A noun is needed after 'form of'; 'relax' becomes 'relaxation' by adding the suffix '-ation'.
An adjective is needed before 'cognitive benefits'; 'signify' becomes 'significant' by changing '-ify' to '-icant'.
An adjective is needed before 'and rewarding'; 'enjoy' becomes 'enjoyable' by adding the suffix '-able'.
